forecasts.reasoning: Articles from July 2025 (e.g. July 4 and July 20) explicitly report that Beto O'Rourke remained undecided on running for the U.S. Senate seat. By November 2025 he said he was 'unlikely' to run, and by January 2026 news updates indicate he was refusing to take sides in the Democratic Senate primary between Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico. Thus, it is practically certain that he did not announce his candidacy before the July 1, 2025 deadline.

Multiple sources confirm that a major conflict occurred between India and Pakistan in May 2025, sparked by an April 22 terror attack in Pahalgam. The kinetic phase of this conflict took place over four days, from May 6 to May 10, 2025. On May 10, a ceasefire mediated by the United States was announced. Post-conflict analyses from June, July, and August 2025 uniformly refer to the event as the “May 2025 India-Pakistan Conflict” or “Four Days in May.” Although there were reports of the ceasefire fraying or being violated shortly after it was signed, there is no evidence of Indian kinetic attacks extending into June, nor operations deep into Pakistan proper (Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa) during the month of June. Official statements confirm that fighting effectively halted on May 10 after India struck several Pakistani airfields. Given the retroactive consistency in naming this a purely May conflict and the absence of any reports indicating a resumption of strikes in June, the probability of an Indian kinetic attack in Pakistan proper in June 2025 is extremely low.

The base rate of a pilot reporting a UFO in an African country in any single specific month to the US-based National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) is astronomically low (estimated to be well under 1%). General web searches for such an event in June 2025 yielded no relevant news reports or discussions, which often surface when pilots publicly report UAPs. Given the lack of positive evidence and the extremely low base rate, the probability is set to the minimum allowed value of 0.02.

Multiple sources published significantly after the resolution date (July 1, 2025) confirm that Rippling did not announce plans for an IPO or file an S-1 before this date. A May 9, 2025, CNBC article quotes CEO Parker Conrad stating the company "isn't planning for an IPO in the near future." Furthermore, articles from December 2025 and early 2026 uniformly state that Rippling has not yet announced an IPO, remains a private company, and has not officially filed an S-1 with the SEC. Given that the resolution date has already passed and the consensus of subsequent reporting is that no such announcement occurred, I am highly confident that the criteria for a positive resolution were not met.

The question asks if the government of Mali announced a reversal of its May 13, 2025 decree dissolving all political parties before July 1, 2025. Search results indicate that the decree remained fully in effect through June and July 2025. On June 17, 2025, the European Parliament passed a resolution urging the Malian president to reverse the decree, indicating no reversal had occurred by then. In late August 2025, a Malian court ruled to stay the execution of the decree, further demonstrating that the government had not voluntarily reversed its decision prior to that ruling, let alone prior to July 1. Therefore, the outcome is clearly No.

The question asks if Databricks will announce before July 1, 2025 that it is planning an IPO, either via an SEC Form S-1 filing or an announcement on the news section of its website. Multiple reliable and up-to-date sources from late 2025 and 2026 clearly state that Databricks had not filed an S-1 or announced any solid timeline for an IPO as of mid-2025. A late 2025 article directly states that Databricks did not have an IPO on the calendar as of mid-2025. Moreover, a comprehensive VC article updated in April 2026 verifies that Databricks has not filed an S-1, nor announced a confidential S-1. Since the forecast date is April 2026 and the resolution date was July 2025, it is clear in hindsight that no such event occurred before July 1, 2025. Therefore, the probability of this resolving YES is extremely close to 0. I will select the lowest allowed threshold, which is 0.02.

Because we are past the resolution date (July 1, 2025) and no news or search results indicate either Donald Trump or Elon Musk posted a new tweet containing the exact phrase "diversity visa" in 2025. While Donald Trump did tweet the phrase "Diversity Visa Lottery Program" in 2017—which would show up in the resolution search criteria—Metaculus rules and precedents for 'Will [X] do [Y]' questions mandate that the event must occur after the question's launch date. Users typically flag such discrepancies early, leading to admin clarifications that old tweets do not count. Given the lack of a new qualifying tweet, the question would have safely resolved as NO.
